Government Agencies and Bodies.label: CMS
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, an agency within HHS.
terms: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: FDA
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Food and Drug Administration, an agency within HHS.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: Congress
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The legislative branch of the U.S. federal government.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: AHRQ
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality, an agency within HHS.
terms: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: DEA
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Drug Enforcement Administration, responsible for enforcing controlled substances laws.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: CDC
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, a national public health agency.
terms: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: OMB
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Office of Management and Budget, which oversees the implementation of the President's vision across the Executive Branch.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: MAC
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: Medicare Administrative Contractor, a private health care insurer that has been awarded a geographic jurisdiction to process Medicare Part A and Part B medical claims.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: OIG
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Office of Inspector General for the Department of Health and Human Services.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: NIH
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The National Institutes of Health, the primary agency for biomedical and public health research.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: OHS
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Office of Head Start, within the Administration for Children and Families.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: CMMI
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Center for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation, a division within CMS that tests innovative payment and service delivery models.
terms: Center for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: OCR
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Office for Civil Rights within HHS, responsible for enforcing civil rights laws.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: CDER
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Center for Drug Evaluation and Research, a center within the FDA.
terms: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: DOJ
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The U.S. Department of Justice, a federal executive department responsible for the enforcement of the law and administration of justice.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: VA
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs, which provides healthcare services to eligible military veterans.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: ASTP / ONC
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy (formerly the Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology), responsible for nationwide health information technology efforts.
terms: Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: SAMHSA
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration, an agency within HHS.
terms: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: CBER
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research, a center within the FDA.
terms: Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: ACF
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Administration for Children and Families, a division of HHS.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: CDRH
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Center for Devices and Radiological Health, a center within the FDA.
terms: Center for Devices and Radiological Health

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: HRSA
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Health Resources and Services Administration, an agency of HHS for improving health care to people who are geographically isolated, economically or medically vulnerable.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: FTC
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Federal Trade Commission, an independent agency that administers antitrust and consumer protection legislation.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: MedPAC
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Medicare Payment Advisory Commission, an independent congressional agency advising the U.S. Congress on issues affecting the Medicare program.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: CTP
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Center for Tobacco Products, a center within the FDA.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: NIST
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The National Institute of Standards and Technology, a non-regulatory agency of the U.S. Department of Commerce.
terms: National Institute of Standards and Technology

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: CVM
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Center for Veterinary Medicine, a center within the FDA.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: IHS
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Indian Health Service, an operating division within HHS.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: OSHA
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Occupational Safety and Health Administration, a regulatory agency of the U.S. Department of Labor.

Healthcare Entities and Providers.label: Pharmacy Benefit Manager
Healthcare Entities and Providers.definition: A third-party administrator of prescription drug programs for commercial health plans, self-insured employers, Medicare Part D plans, and government employee plans.

Healthcare Entities and Providers.label: Skilled Nursing Facility
Healthcare Entities and Providers.definition: A health-care institution that meets federal criteria for accreditation and is staffed with trained medical professionals.

Healthcare Entities and Providers.label: Critical Access Hospital
Healthcare Entities and Providers.definition: A designation given to eligible rural hospitals by CMS to reduce the financial vulnerability of rural hospitals and improve access to healthcare.

Healthcare Entities and Providers.label: FQHC
Healthcare Entities and Providers.definition: Federally Qualified Health Center, a community-based health care provider that receives funds from the HRSA Health Center Program.

Healthcare Entities and Providers.label: Advanced Practice Registered Nurse
Healthcare Entities and Providers.definition: A nurse who has obtained at least a master's degree in nursing, including CNPs, CNSs, CNMs, and CRNAs.

Healthcare Entities and Providers.label: Long-Term Care Facility
Healthcare Entities and Providers.definition: A facility that provides rehabilitative, restorative, and/or ongoing skilled nursing care to patients or residents in need of assistance with activities of daily living.

Healthcare Entities and Providers.label: Direct Support Professional
Healthcare Entities and Providers.definition: A person who works directly with people with physical and/or intellectual disabilities with the aim of assisting the individual to become integrated into their community.

Healthcare Entities and Providers.label: Certified Registered Nurse Anesthetist
Healthcare Entities and Providers.definition: An advanced practice registered nurse who administers anesthesia for surgery or other medical procedures.

Healthcare Entities and Providers.label: Rural Emergency Hospital
Healthcare Entities and Providers.definition: A new Medicare provider type intended to maintain access to emergency and outpatient services in rural areas.

Healthcare Entities and Providers.label: Rural Health Clinic
Healthcare Entities and Providers.definition: A clinic certified to receive special Medicare and Medicaid reimbursement to encourage the delivery of primary care services in underserved rural areas.

Healthcare Entities and Providers.label: QIDP
Healthcare Entities and Providers.definition: Qualified Intellectual Disability Professional, a staff member responsible for integrating, coordinating, and monitoring individuals' active treatment programs in an ICF/IID.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Medicaid
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: A joint federal and state program that helps with medical costs for some people with limited income and resources.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Head Start
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: A federal program that promotes school readiness of children from birth to age five from low-income families.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Early Head Start
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: A federal program serving infants, toddlers, and pregnant women and their families who have incomes below the Federal poverty level.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Medicare
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: A federal health insurance program for people 65 or older, certain younger people with disabilities, and people with End-Stage Renal Disease.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Medicare Advantage
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: A type of Medicare health plan (Part C) offered by a private company that contracts with Medicare to provide Part A and Part B benefits.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Home Health Benefit
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: A range of health care services that can be given in a patient's home for an illness or injury.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Medicare Part D
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: The part of Medicare that helps cover the cost of prescription drugs.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: MIPS
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: The Merit-based Incentive Payment System, one of two tracks under the Quality Payment Program.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Medicare Part B
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: The part of Medicare that covers certain doctors' services, outpatient care, medical supplies, and preventive services.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Physician Fee Schedule
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: A complete listing of fees used by Medicare to pay doctors or other suppliers/providers.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Promoting Interoperability
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: A performance category within MIPS that promotes patient engagement and electronic exchange of health information using certified EHR technology.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: CHIP
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: The Children's Health Insurance Program, providing low-cost health coverage to children in families who earn too much money to qualify for Medicaid.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Medicare FFS
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: The traditional Medicare program where the government pays directly for healthcare services.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Quality Payment Program
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: A CMS program that rewards high-value, high-quality Medicare clinicians with payment increases.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Star Ratings Program
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: A quality rating system used by CMS to measure the performance of Medicare Advantage and Part D plans.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Medicare Shared Savings
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: A program that encourages groups of doctors, hospitals, and other health care providers to come together as an ACO to give coordinated, high-quality care to their Medicare beneficiaries.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: 340B Program
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: A federal drug pricing program that requires drug manufacturers to provide outpatient drugs to eligible health care organizations at significantly reduced prices.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: Alternative Payment Model
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: A payment approach that gives added incentive payments to provide high-quality and cost-efficient care.

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: ESRD PPS
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: The End-Stage Renal Disease Prospective Payment System, a bundled payment system for Medicare dialysis services.
terms: End Stage Renal Disease Prospective Payment System

Healthcare Programs and Systems.label: WIC
Healthcare Programs and Systems.definition: The Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children, a federal assistance program.
terms: Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children

Key Concepts and Principles.label: Interoperability
Key Concepts and Principles.definition: The ability of different information systems, devices, and applications to access, exchange, integrate, and cooperatively use data in a coordinated manner.

Key Concepts and Principles.label: Value-Based Care
Key Concepts and Principles.definition: A healthcare delivery model in which providers are paid based on patient health outcomes, rather than the quantity of services delivered.

Key Concepts and Principles.label: Home and Community-Based Services
Key Concepts and Principles.definition: Services and supports provided to people in their own homes or other residential settings, rather than in institutions.

Key Concepts and Principles.label: Scope of Practice
Key Concepts and Principles.definition: The procedures, actions, and processes that a healthcare practitioner is permitted to undertake in keeping with the terms of their professional license.

Key Concepts and Principles.label: Information Blocking
Key Concepts and Principles.definition: A practice that is likely to interfere with, prevent, or materially discourage access, exchange, or use of electronic health information.

Key Concepts and Principles.label: Health Equity
Key Concepts and Principles.definition: The state in which everyone has a fair and just opportunity to attain their highest level of health.

Key Concepts and Principles.label: Drug Shortage
Key Concepts and Principles.definition: A period when the demand or projected demand for a drug within the United States exceeds the supply of the drug.

Key Concepts and Principles.label: Price Transparency
Key Concepts and Principles.definition: The practice of making pricing information available to consumers, intended to allow them to shop for care and make informed decisions.

Key Concepts and Principles.label: Full Practice Authority
Key Concepts and Principles.definition: The authorization of nurse practitioners to evaluate patients, diagnose, order and interpret diagnostic tests, and initiate and manage treatments under the exclusive licensure authority of the state board of nursing.

Key Concepts and Principles.label: Surprise Billing
Key Concepts and Principles.definition: An unexpected bill from an out-of-network provider or facility.

Key Concepts and Principles.label: Essential Health Benefits
Key Concepts and Principles.definition: A set of 10 categories of services that health insurance plans must cover under the Affordable Care Act.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: ADHD
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder, a chronic condition including attention difficulty, hyperactivity, and impulsiveness.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: Substance Use Disorder
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: A disease that affects a person's brain and behavior and leads to an inability to control the use of a legal or illegal drug or medicine.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: COVID-19
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: A contagious disease caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: Cancer
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: A disease in which some of the body's cells grow uncontrollably and spread to other parts of the body.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: Insomnia
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: A common sleep disorder that can make it hard to fall asleep, hard to stay asleep, or cause you to wake up too early and not be able to get back to sleep.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: Diabetes
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: A chronic health condition that affects how the body turns food into energy.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: Obesity
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: A complex disease involving an excessive amount of body fat.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: Depression
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: A mood disorder causing a persistent feeling of sadness and loss of interest.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: Intellectual/Developmental Disability
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: Disorders that are usually present at birth and that uniquely affect the trajectory of the individual’s physical, intellectual, and/or emotional development.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: Influenza
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: A viral infection that attacks the respiratory system.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: RSV
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: Respiratory Syncytial Virus, a common respiratory virus that usually causes mild, cold-like symptoms.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: Havana Syndrome
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: A set of medical signs and symptoms reported by U.S. and Canadian embassy staff, also known as Anomalous Health Incidents.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: Opioid Use Disorder
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: A problematic pattern of opioid use that causes significant impairment or distress.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: Chronic Kidney Disease
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: A condition characterized by a gradual loss of kidney function over time.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: ESRD
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: End-Stage Renal Disease, the last stage of chronic kidney disease when kidneys can no longer support the body's needs.

Medical Conditions and Diseases.label: PTSD
Medical Conditions and Diseases.definition: Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder, a mental health condition that's triggered by a terrifying event.

Medical Devices and Equipment.label: Medical Device
Medical Devices and Equipment.definition: An instrument, apparatus, or other article intended for use in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease.

Medical Devices and Equipment.label: Durable Medical Equipment
Medical Devices and Equipment.definition: Equipment that can withstand repeated use, is primarily used for a medical reason, and is appropriate for use in the home.

Medical Devices and Equipment.label: Device Classification
Medical Devices and Equipment.definition: A three-tiered system (Class I, II, III) used by the FDA to categorize medical devices based on their risk to the patient.

Medical Devices and Equipment.label: In Vitro Diagnostic
Medical Devices and Equipment.definition: Tests done on samples such as blood or tissue that have been taken from the human body.

Medical Devices and Equipment.label: Software as a Medical Device
Medical Devices and Equipment.definition: Software intended to be used for one or more medical purposes that perform these purposes without being part of a hardware medical device.

Medications and Treatments.label: Telehealth
Medications and Treatments.definition: The use of electronic information and telecommunications technologies to support long-distance clinical health care.

Medications and Treatments.label: Drug Schedules
Medications and Treatments.definition: A classification system for drugs based on their abuse potential, established by the Controlled Substances Act.

Medications and Treatments.label: Generic Drug
Medications and Treatments.definition: A medication created to be the same as an already marketed brand-name drug in dosage form, safety, strength, route of administration, quality, performance characteristics, and intended use.

Medications and Treatments.label: Opioids
Medications and Treatments.definition: A class of drugs used to reduce pain, which can be highly addictive.

Medications and Treatments.label: Controlled Substance
Medications and Treatments.definition: A drug or chemical whose manufacture, possession, or use is regulated by a government.

Medications and Treatments.label: Stimulants
Medications and Treatments.definition: A class of drugs that increase activity in the brain, often used to treat ADHD.

Medications and Treatments.label: Biosimilar
Medications and Treatments.definition: A biologic medical product that is almost an identical copy of an original product that is manufactured by a different company.

Medications and Treatments.label: Cell and Gene Therapy
Medications and Treatments.definition: Overlapping fields of biomedical research and treatment with the goals of repairing or replacing damaged cells or correcting genetic disorders.

Medications and Treatments.label: Compounded Medication
Medications and Treatments.definition: A drug that is made by a licensed pharmacist to meet the unique needs of an individual patient.

Medications and Treatments.label: GLP-1 Agonists
Medications and Treatments.definition: A class of medications used in the treatment of type 2 diabetes and obesity.

Medications and Treatments.label: DSMT
Medications and Treatments.definition: Diabetes self-management training, an evidence-based program to help people with diabetes manage their condition.

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: Executive Order
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: A signed, written, and published directive from the President of the United States that manages operations of the federal government.

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: HIPAA
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: The Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, which sets standards for protecting sensitive patient health information.
terms: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: Affordable Care Act
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: The Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act, a comprehensive health care reform law enacted in 2010.
terms: Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: FD&C Act
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: The Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act, a set of laws giving authority to the FDA to oversee the safety of food, drugs, medical devices, and cosmetics.
terms: Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: Social Security Act
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: The 1935 law that created the Social Security program and has been amended to form the basis of many health and human services programs.

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: 21st Century Cures Act
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: A 2016 act designed to help accelerate medical product development and bring new innovations and advances to patients who need them faster and more efficiently.

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: Anti-Kickback Statute
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: A federal statute that prohibits the exchange of remuneration for referrals for services payable by a federal healthcare program.

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: Stark Law
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: A set of U.S. federal laws that prohibit physician self-referral for designated health services payable by Medicare or Medicaid.

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: No Surprises Act
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: A federal law that protects people covered under group and individual health plans from receiving surprise medical bills.

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: FSMA
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: The Food Safety Modernization Act, which aims to ensure the U.S. food supply is safe by shifting the focus from responding to contamination to preventing it.

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: CARES Act
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: The Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act, a 2020 law intended to address the economic fallout of the COVID-19 pandemic.

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: 80/20 Rule
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: A mandate requiring at least 80% of Medicaid payments for certain HCBS services be spent on compensation for direct care workers.

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: Common Rule
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: A rule of ethics in the United States regarding biomedical and behavioral research involving human subjects (45 CFR 46).

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: MHPAEA
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: The Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act of 2008, a federal law that prevents group health plans and health insurance issuers that provide mental health or substance use disorder benefits from imposing less favorable benefit limitations on those benefits than on medical/surgical benefits.
terms: Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: Controlled Substances Act
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: The federal U.S. drug policy under which the manufacture, importation, possession, use, and distribution of certain substances is regulated.

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: DSHEA
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: The Dietary Supplement Health and Education Act of 1994, which defines and regulates dietary supplements.
terms: Dietary Supplement Health and Education Act

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: NBPP
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: The annual Notice of Benefit and Payment Parameters rule, which finalizes regulations for the ACA marketplaces.
terms: Notice of Benefit and Payment Parameters

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: 42 CFR Part 2
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: A federal regulation that protects the confidentiality of substance use disorder patient records.

Regulations, Acts, and Policies.label: DQSA
Regulations, Acts, and Policies.definition: The Drug Quality and Security Act, which outlines steps to build a system to identify and trace certain prescription drugs as they are distributed in the United States.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: Rulemaking
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: The process that executive and independent agencies use to create, or promulgate, regulations.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: Prior Authorization
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: A process requiring providers to obtain approval from a payer before a specific service or medication is covered.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: Audits
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: An official inspection of an organization's accounts, typically by an independent body, to ensure compliance with regulations.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: Clinical Trials
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: Research studies performed in people that are aimed at evaluating a medical, surgical, or behavioral intervention.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: Credentialing
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: The process of verifying a healthcare provider's qualifications to ensure they have the required licenses, certifications, and skills.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: New Drug Application
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: A formal proposal that a sponsor submits to the FDA to request approval for the sale and marketing of a new drug.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: Risk Adjustment
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: A statistical process that takes into account the underlying health status and health spending of enrollees in an insurance plan when looking at their health care outcomes or costs.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: Coverage with Evidence Development
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: A CMS process that allows for Medicare coverage of items and services on the condition that they are furnished in the context of approved clinical studies or with the collection of additional clinical data.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: Price Negotiation
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: The process established by the Inflation Reduction Act for Medicare to negotiate prices for certain high-cost drugs.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: Independent Dispute Resolution
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: A process under the No Surprises Act to resolve payment disputes between providers and health plans for out-of-network services.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: Premarket Approval
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: The FDA process of scientific and regulatory review to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of Class III medical devices.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: Electronic Visit Verification
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: A technology system that verifies when provider visits occur and documents the precise time services begin and end.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: Abbreviated New Drug Application
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: An application for a generic drug approval, containing data that is submitted to FDA for the review and potential approval of a generic drug product.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: Step Therapy
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: A type of prior authorization where a patient must try a lower-cost drug before a plan will cover a more expensive one.

Regulatory and Administrative Processes.label: RADV Audits
Regulatory and Administrative Processes.definition: Risk Adjustment Data Validation audits, which CMS uses to verify the accuracy of diagnosis data submitted by Medicare Advantage organizations.

Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.label: EHR / EMR
Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.definition: Electronic Health Record or Electronic Medical Record, a digital version of a patient's paper chart.

Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.label: Guidance for Industry
Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.definition: Documents prepared by the FDA to provide their current thinking on a regulatory issue.

Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.label: CPT Codes
Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.definition: Current Procedural Terminology, a medical code set that is used to report medical, surgical, and diagnostic procedures and services.

Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.label: HCPCS
Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.definition: The Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System, a collection of standardized codes that represent medical procedures, supplies, products and services.

Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.label: National Provider Identifier
Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.definition: A unique 10-digit identification number issued to health care providers in the United States by CMS.

Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.label: NHSN
Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.definition: The National Healthcare Safety Network, a healthcare-associated infection tracking system from the CDC.

Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.label: FHIR
Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.definition: Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources, a standard for exchanging healthcare information electronically.

Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.label: CLASS Scoring System
Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.definition: The Classroom Assessment Scoring System, an observational tool that assesses classroom quality in preschool through third grade.

Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.label: OASIS Data Set
Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.definition: The Outcome and Assessment Information Set, a group of standard data elements home health agencies must collect and report to CMS.

Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.label: TEFCA
Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.definition: The Trusted Exchange Framework and Common Agreement, a unified approach to national-level health information exchange.
terms: Trusted Exchange Framework and Common Agreement

Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.label: USP
Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.definition: The U.S. Pharmacopeia, a scientific nonprofit organization that sets standards for the identity, strength, quality, and purity of medicines, food ingredients, and dietary supplements.

Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.label: CEHRT
Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.definition: Certified Electronic Health Record Technology, an EHR that meets functionality standards set by the Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT.

Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.label: Federal Poverty Line
Standards, Guidelines, and Data Systems.definition: A measure of income issued every year by the Department of Health and Human Services, used to determine eligibility for certain programs and benefits.
